skill.name: create-worktree-skill
skill.description: Creates isolated git worktrees through a slash command, with separate ports, environment files, dependency setup, and service startup for parallel development.
skill.summary: Create isolated git worktrees for parallel development environments.
skill.author: EGAdams

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives from markdown examples and descriptive text. The skill is still medium risk because its intended slash command creates worktrees, writes environment files, installs dependencies, starts services, and may kill ports.

medium_findings.title: Slash Command Performs Filesystem and Process Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run a slash command that creates a git worktree, writes environment files, installs dependencies, starts services, and may kill occupied ports. This appears legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can modify the local repository and running processes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly described in the skill instructions. The risk is operational rather than malicious because no unauthorized network endpoint, credential access, or prompt injection is present.

medium_findings.title: User Branch Name Is Passed to Worktree Command
medium_findings.description: The skill extracts a user-provided branch name and passes it to the worktree slash command. The skill does not show validation logic, so safety depends on the slash command implementation outside this file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from user request to command argument is clear. The actual command implementation is not included, so injection risk cannot be confirmed from this file alone.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown code fences and inline command examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation for invoking a slash command, not executable Ruby code in this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside markdown prose, examples, or a fenced block. No Ruby source file or executable backtick expression exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not reference a cryptographic algorithm or implement cryptographic behavior. No evidence found for MD5, SHA-1, or another weak algorithm in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain skill metadata and a statement about error messages. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashes, or algorithm names.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The flagged line describes isolated port configuration for local worktrees. No evidence found of host enumeration, network scanning, or reconnaissance behavior in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a feature list for local port isolation. Related text mentions avoiding port conflicts, which is expected for parallel development environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Delegation to SlashCommand
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill delegates setup to `/create_worktree_prompt` with user-supplied branch names. Review the slash command implementation before publishing in high-trust environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command delegation is directly documented. The scanned file does not include the delegated command implementation, so the assessment is limited to visible instructions.

content.value_statement: Parallel development often causes port, database, and configuration conflicts. This skill creates isolated git worktree environments through a dedicated slash command.
actual_capabilities: Invokes `/create_worktree_prompt` for a requested branch name.
Supports an optional port offset for predictable local ports.
Explains generated access URLs, port assignments, and management steps.
Handles multiple requested branches by invoking the command for each branch.
Documents common failure causes for worktree creation and startup.
limitations: Requires the `/create_worktree_prompt` slash command to exist in the host environment.
Does not include the slash command implementation in this skill package.
Requires git worktree support and expected project dependencies such as bun.
May modify local files and running services through the delegated command.

use_cases.title: Feature Branch Isolation
use_cases.description: Create a separate worktree for a feature branch without disrupting the main checkout.

use_cases.title: Parallel Review Environments
use_cases.description: Run several branch environments side by side with separate ports for comparison and review.

use_cases.title: Hotfix Testing
use_cases.description: Start an isolated hotfix branch environment while active development continues elsewhere.

prompt_templates.title: Create One Worktree
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create worktree skill for branch feature-dashboard.

prompt_templates.title: Set a Port Offset
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create worktree skill for branch hotfix-login with port offset 5.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Worktrees
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare several branches for parallel work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create worktree skill for branches feature-a, feature-b, and feature-c.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Review Environments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create worktree skill to set up review environments for feature-search and feature-billing with unique ports.

output_examples.input: Use the create worktree skill for branch feature-dashboard.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the worktree slash command for feature-dashboard, then reports the created directory, assigned ports, access URLs, and cleanup instructions.

output_examples.input: Use the create worktree skill for hotfix-security with port offset 5.
output_examples.output: The assistant invokes the slash command with the branch and offset, then summarizes the isolated ports and service status.

output_examples.input: Use the create worktree skill for feature-a and feature-b.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the command once for each branch and explains the separate worktree paths and local environment details.

best_practices: Confirm the branch name before invoking the skill if the request is ambiguous.
Use a port offset when several local environments are already running.
Review the slash command output before sharing URLs or cleanup steps.
anti_patterns: Do not invoke this skill unless the user explicitly asks to use a skill for worktree creation.
Do not use untrusted branch names without reviewing the delegated slash command behavior.
Do not run it in repositories where local file changes or service restarts are unacceptable.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a git worktree environment through the `/create_worktree_prompt` slash command.

faq.question: Does it choose ports automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill says the slash command assigns isolated ports unless the user provides a port offset.

faq.question: Can it create several worktrees?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the assistant to run the slash command once for each requested branch.

faq.question: Does this package include the slash command code?
faq.answer: No. The scanned file documents how to invoke the command but does not include its implementation.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It expects SlashCommand access and a project environment that supports git worktrees and dependency installation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it performs local setup through a privileged command.
